title: Verilator allows function outputs to be wires
body:
The following code compiles with no errors or warnings:

```
module main(
    output wire bit valid,
    output wire bit ready
);
    import "DPI-C" context function void example_dpi(output bit valid);

    function void example_sv(output bit ready);
        ready = 1;
    endfunction

    initial begin
        example_dpi(valid);
        example_sv(ready);
    end

endmodule
```

However it doesn't make any sense does it? A function output can't be connected to a `wire`; only a `var`. The LRM says:

> If the argument in the subroutine is declared as an output or an inout, then the corresponding expression
in the subroutine call shall be restricted to an expression that is valid on the left-hand side of a procedural
assignment.

And

> The left-hand side of a procedural assignment can take one of the following forms:
>  * Singular variables, as described in 6.4
>  * Aggregate variables, as described in Clause 7
>  * Bit-selects, part-selects, and slices of packed arrays
>  * Slices of unpacked arrays

I'm using Verilator 5.004 2022-12-14 rev v5.004.
